L1.area: User-facing account management theme resources and client-side rendering layer

L2.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-supplied profile data fields are not properly encoded before being rendered in the account management interface. An attacker can craft profile field values containing malicious JavaScript that executes when any user views the affected page, escalating to a complete account takeover. The highest threat is to data confidentiality, integrity, and system availability.

L3.area: Keycloak v2 account theme client-side scripts for the welcome and landing pages
L3.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user profile fields such as display name components are not sanitized before being rendered in the Keycloak v2 account management console. An attacker can inject malicious JavaScript into their profile data that executes when any user views the account welcome page, enabling complete account takeover.

ground_truth.reason: The loggedInUserName function in the account welcome page returns user-supplied profile fields (given_name, family_name, preferred_username) without HTML encoding, allowing stored XSS via crafted profile data.
